video:video.video:title: Citigroup and the 'financial supermarket' experiment | FT Film
video:video.video:description: The 1998 mega-merger between banking giant Citicorp and insurer Travelers Group created the world's biggest financial services company. video:video.video:title: Undervaluing climate risk | FT Moral Money
video:video.video:description: The world is reeling from record-breaking heatwaves, wildfires, rainfall and devastating floods. As well as the human toll, these can also be measured in the economic value destroyed. In a world that is rapidly becoming more vulnerable to extreme weather events, outdated assumptions about asset values need recalibrating

video:video.video:title: Is ultra-processed food really that bad? | FT Food Revolution
video:video.video:description: Ultra-processed foods are cheap and convenient. But many health experts say they are simply bad for us, and, as the FTs Madeleine Speed reports, health warnings about UPFs could have unintended consequences. Governments are mulling over how to advocate for more balanced diets, but kicking the ultra-processed habit may take some time

video:video.video:title: The creator economy is changing, but can it thrive? | FT Tech
video:video.video:description: The number of online creators active today is staggering, and the creator economy is valued at some $250bn by Goldman Sachs. But, as the FT’s Cristina Criddle explains, one key problem is that traffic and wealth tends to be concentrated among the very few. The industry has received mixed financial signals recently, so who’s actually making money and what does the future hold?

Could modular, wooden turbines help address those challenges?
